title: Software Engineer, Forward Deployed AI
company_focus:
Automating corporate spend management and financial
infrastructure
description:
About Ramp

Ramp is building the smart infrastructure for finance teams,
embedded in the transaction flow of every dollar a business
spends. We automate how over $100B in annualized spend flows
in and out of 50,000+ companies: authorizing payments,
flagging risk, categorizing spend, and closing books.

The problems are high-stakes, data-dense, and unforgiving.

We hire people with high agency and high urgency. We look
for slope over intercept. We care less about where you
trained and more about what you’ve built. At Ramp, everyone
is a builder who owns problems end to end and makes
consequential decisions that shape the outcome.

The median Ramp customer saves 5% and grows revenue 16% in
their first year – far in excess of businesses operating
without Ramp. We believe every ambitious company deserves
the same.

If you want to build systems that directly shape how
companies move and manage billions, Ramp is the place to do
it.

About the Role

Being a part of Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE), you will
design and build solutions to support our largest customers
and scale our enterprise motion. FDE is a high-pace,
customer-facing, business-focused engineering team, and we
pave the way for Ramp to support the largest and most
complex organizations in the world.

On FDE, you will interact directly with customers and
deliver solutions end to end — from understanding pain
points and shaping product decisions to designing and
implementing scalable systems. We are a full stack team and
collaborate closely with sales and core product engineering.

Check out our Engineering Blog for more on our tech stack,
mission and values!

What You’ll Do

- Deliver solutions end to end that meet the needs of our
  largest, strategic customers — understanding user pain
  points, scoping product specs, and designing and building
  software.
- Collaborate closely with our sales and go-to-market teams to
  close exciting deals, activate customers, and expand the
  value Ramp provides over time. We work with and support
  huge, world-class companies at the scale of $100B+ in
  valuation.
- Drive our core product engineering roadmap and make
  important prioritization and scoping decisions that
  determine when we build custom solutions vs. accelerate
  larger existing projects. Our team uses an embedding model
  where FDEs embed within core product engineering teams to
  develop expertise in the most relevant technical areas.
- Solve technical challenges to support new levels of scale,
  and help us develop and execute our strategy to grow our
  enterprise platform. For example, extending our data model
  for roles and permissions, seamlessly integrating with 3rd
  party systems with generalized data exports, and ingesting
  HRIS data at scale.

What You Need

- A minimum of 2 years of software engineering experience
  preferred
- Track record of shipping high-quality products and features
  at scale
- Ability to turn business and product ideas into engineering
  solutions
- Desire to work in a fast-paced environment, continuously
  grow, and master your craft
- Demonstrated hands-on, daily use of AI coding agents as the
  primary development driver.
- Comfort with agentic workflows, orchestration layers, and AI
  tooling broadly

Benefits (for U.S.-based full-time employees)

- 100% medical, dental & vision insurance coverage for you
  - Partially covered for your dependents
  - One Medical annual membership
- 401k (including employer match on contributions made while
  employed by Ramp)
- Flexible PTO
- Fertility HRA (up to $10,000 per year)
- Parental Leave
- Unlimited AI token usage
- Pet insurance
- Centralized home-office equipment ordering for all employees
- Health and Wellness stipend
- In-office perks: lunch, snacks, drinks, and more
- Budget for intra-office travel
- Relocation support to NYC or SF (as needed)

Referral Instructions

If you are being referred for the role, please contact that
person to apply on your behalf.

Other notices

Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, we will
consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and
conviction records.

Beware of recruiting scams: Ramp will only contact you
through official @Ramp.com email addresses and will never
ask for payment or sensitive personal information during the
hiring process.

Ramp Applicant Privacy Notice
responsibilities: Design and build end-to-end solutions for Ramp's largest strategic customers, from understanding pain points to shipping software
Collaborate with sales and go-to-market teams to close deals, activate customers, and expand Ramp's value proposition
Drive core product engineering roadmap by making prioritization and scoping decisions between custom solutions and broader product features
Embed within core product engineering teams to develop deep expertise in relevant technical areas
Solve technical scaling challenges including extending data models, integrating with third-party systems, and ingesting HRIS data at scale
Use AI coding agents and agentic workflows as the primary development approach for building and delivering solutions
use_cases: Enterprise spend management and financial automation for large organizations
Third-party system integration with generalized data exports at scale
Roles and permissions data model extension for complex enterprise hierarchies
HRIS data ingestion at scale for enterprise customer onboarding
Risk flagging and payment authorization for high-volume corporate transactions
ai_classification_reasoning:
The role title explicitly includes "Forward Deployed AI" and
requires demonstrated hands-on daily use of AI coding agents
as the primary development driver, plus comfort with agentic
workflows, orchestration layers, and AI tooling broadly. The
engineer builds AI-driven solutions end-to-end for Ramp's
largest enterprise customers, making this an ai-first role
focused on deploying AI-powered systems.
